title: gladiolus hajastanicus
intro: Gladiolus hajastanicus, also known as Armenian Gladiolus, is a species of flowering plant in the iris family, native to Armenia and surrounding regions, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, such as inflammation and ulcers. The health benefits of Gladiolus hajastanicus include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Gladiolus hajastanicus can be made by drying the flowers and roots, and then infusing them in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves for topical use.
